body.value: 鹿児島藩 — 書ナラス（かきならず）＝「記載なし／書面に出さない」

Kagoshima (Satsuma) domain — Table 8 entry. Its ENTIRE military disclosure is the two-character note 書ナラス ('not written / not specified'). A near-total refusal: combined with Satsuma's actual ~16,000-Spencer-rifle force, this made it the most opaque military presence in the 1870 survey. Everything to the RIGHT of this ○ marker (read first, right-to-left) is the tail of the PREVIOUS domain — Kanazawa — not Satsuma.
隊士 四百六十四人 ／ 士等役員 千百六人 ／ 兵卒人員 千五百六十八人
これは前の藩（金澤藩）のデータ — 隊士464・士等役員1106・兵卒人員1568・参事47

NOT Satsuma's. These figures (隊士 464, 士等役員 1,106, 兵卒人員 1,568, plus the four councillor counts summing to 47 over at the upper left) sit to the RIGHT of the ○鹿兒島藩 marker, so in right-to-left reading order they belong to the PRECEDING domain — 金澤藩 (Kanazawa) — whose entry continues onto this page from the prior PDF page. They were mistakenly tagged to Satsuma until MASTER was corrected on 2026-05-28.
高知藩（こうちはん）＝土佐藩 — 次の藩の始まり

Kōchi (Tosa) domain — the NEXT domain's entry begins here, to the left of Satsuma's marker. Unlike Satsuma, Tosa discloses a full named officer roster below (知事・大参事・権大参事… 深尾弘人, 五藤如山, 齋藤彌九馬…). Its presence confirms that the block between the two ○ markers — Satsuma's — contains only 書ナラス.
